pi_items.source: export_result.sh line 11: IMAGE_NAME="secure-app:latest"; setup_task.sh line 110: docker rmi secure-app:latest
setup_task.sh line 38: PORT = 8090 (in Python server); setup_task.sh line 65: fuser -k 8090/tcp
export_result.sh line 34: docker run --rm $IMAGE_NAME test -f /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz; setup_task.sh line 76: WORKDIR /app in Dockerfile
setup_task.sh line 10: TOKEN=$(openssl rand -hex 16); stored in /tmp/secret_token.txt and $PROJECT_DIR/auth_token.txt
setup_task.sh lines 25-28: Creates synthetic artifact as 'This is the proprietary library content.' + 1024 random bytes from /dev/urandom
setup_task.sh lines 46-52: Python HTTP server checks 'X-Auth-Token' header and returns 403 if invalid

privileged_info_summary: The task is a Docker BuildKit security exercise with all synthetic data. The authentication token is randomly generated (32-character hex string using openssl rand -hex 16) at task setup and stored in both /tmp/secret_token.txt (server-side) and auth_token.txt (client-side in /home/ga/secure-build/). The proprietary library artifact is a synthetic dummy file created with text content plus 1024 random bytes from /dev/urandom, served from a temporary Python HTTP server on port 8090 that requires X-Auth-Token header authentication. The Dockerfile is created as an incomplete template that the agent must modify to use Docker BuildKit secrets (RUN --mount=type=secret) to securely download the artifact to /app/proprietary_lib.tar.gz without leaking the token in build history or environment variables.
